description_long: This object appears to be a partially eaten snack, likely a chocolate bar or similar confectionery, with a jagged, broken end and some small fragments scattered nearby. The main body is elongated and slightly curved, with a dark, almost black exterior and a hint of reddish-brown near the broken edge, suggesting a chocolate or candy coating with a possible filling. The broken tip reveals a lighter interior, possibly nougat, caramel, or wafer, and the scattered crumbs reinforce the impression of a brittle, edible item. The surface is matte and not highly reflective, with a subtle texture that suggests a soft or semi-soft bite. No visible branding, text, or graphics are present on the exterior. The object is small enough to be handheld and is not a receptacle or container.
description: A partially eaten, dark-colored snack bar with a jagged broken end and scattered crumbs, showing a reddish-brown edge and a matte, textured surface.
description_view_0: The main body of the snack is visible with a clear view of the broken end and scattered fragments, highlighting the object's length and curved shape.
description_view_1: This view shows the snack from a more top-down angle, emphasizing the broken tip and the distribution of crumbs around it.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the snack is visible, with the broken end facing left and the reddish-brown edge more prominent.
description_view_3: A rear view of the snack, showing the unbroken end and the elongated, slightly curved body with minimal detail on the broken tip.
